tldr: Without live ad data in the scrape, this forecast is built on Roland Mouret's known catalog rotation patterns — and the rotation is thin. Expect CPM creep within 10-14 days unless 2-3 fresh angles enter the set.

benchmark.context: Luxury womenswear brands typically buy more time per creative because the audience is smaller and slower to saturate. Roland Mouret is fatiguing roughly 10 days faster than category median, which signals a rotation gap rather than a targeting problem.

ads.headline: The Galaxy Dress
ads.body: Sculpted to celebrate the female form. Discover the icon, reimagined for the new season.
drivers: Hero product on repeat — Galaxy is the default creative across every set
Copy leans on brand history rather than a buying reason

ads.headline: Tailoring, Reimagined
ads.body: Discover the new season of sculptural silhouettes from Roland Mouret.
drivers: Generic seasonal language with no specific product or proof
No price anchor or occasion framing
Visual direction overlaps with Ad #1 — same mood, same model treatment

ads.headline: The New Collection
ads.body: Discover sculptural tailoring designed for the modern woman.
drivers: Third 'Discover…' headline in the rotation — verbal fatigue
Lookbook stills only, no movement or fabric close-ups

ads.headline: Dress for the Occasion
ads.body: From black-tie to boardroom, Roland Mouret dresses the moments that matter.
drivers: Occasion angle is the freshest in the set — but only one variant
Lacks concrete styling proof (one dress, two looks)
CTA generic — could route to occasionwear edit

ads.headline: Iconic Since 2005
ads.body: Twenty years of the Galaxy. Shop the anniversary edit.
drivers: Heritage angle differentiates but stays in still-image format
Anniversary hook has a natural shelf life
No founder or atelier voice to deepen the story

ads_compact.headline: The Galaxy in Bordeaux
ads_compact.body: The icon, in this season's defining shade.

ads_compact.headline: Sculpted Tailoring
ads_compact.body: Made to move with you. Discover the new arrivals.

ad_to_scale.headline: Dress for the Occasion
ad_to_scale.body: From black-tie to boardroom, Roland Mouret dresses the moments that matter.
ad_to_scale.why: This is the only ad in rotation framing a buying reason rather than a brand mood. Occasion-led creative converts harder in luxury womenswear because it answers 'why now' — scale this one, then build 2-3 occasion-specific variants (gala, wedding-guest, work) off the same skeleton.

ad_to_kill.headline: The New Collection
ad_to_kill.body: Discover sculptural tailoring designed for the modern woman.
ad_to_kill.why: It's the third 'Discover…' headline in your set and adds nothing Ad #1 and Ad #2 don't already say. Cannibalising spend without a unique angle — kill it and reallocate to the occasion variant.

concepts.hook: Each Galaxy takes 14 hours to cut.
concepts.primary_text: The Galaxy is not sewn — it is engineered. Fourteen hours of pattern cutting, hand-finished seams, and the signature folded waistband that has defined Roland Mouret since 2005. Twenty years on, every dress still leaves the atelier the same way: shaped to honour the woman who wears it. Discover the craft behind the icon.
concepts.visual_direction: Slow macro shots of fabric, hands pinning, scissors on toile, the folded waist coming together. Soft studio light, no model.
concepts.fills_gap: No craft or process content in current rotation — every ad is finished-product focused.

concepts.hook: The dress that works Monday to Saturday.
concepts.primary_text: Monday: layered under a tailored coat for the boardroom. Thursday: bare-shouldered with heels for dinner. Saturday: with flats and a trench, because sculptural tailoring doesn't need an occasion. The Galaxy dress, styled three ways — proof that one Roland Mouret piece can carry an entire week of moments.
concepts.visual_direction: Three editorial stills of the same dress on the same model, three contexts (office, restaurant, street). Consistent grading.
concepts.fills_gap: No styling-versatility angle — current ads sell the dress, not the wardrobe logic.

concepts.hook: The dress that built a 20-year red carpet.
concepts.primary_text: Cate. Victoria. Cameron. Scarlett. For two decades, the Galaxy has been the dress women reach for when the cameras are watching. Sculpted, sealed, and unmistakable. The icon returns this season — in three new shades.
concepts.visual_direction: Editorial grid of the dress in three colourways with subtle press-quote overlays, no celebrity imagery (rights-safe).
concepts.fills_gap: Zero social proof in the current set — heritage is implied, never evidenced.

unlocks: Recover ~15-20% CPM headroom by retiring the two 'Discover…' duplicates
Add a craft and a versatility angle — both missing from the current rotation
Extend average creative lifespan from 12 to ~22 days (category median)
Build a testing cadence so the next Galaxy refresh doesn't carry the whole account

next_step.urgency: Ads #1 and #2 fatigue inside 7 days — replacements need to be in flight by next week.
next_step.headline: 30 minutes to map the next 60 days of creative
next_step.body: Let's walk through which of the three concepts to brief first and how to phase the kill-and-scale without dropping spend. You'll leave with a sequenced rollout, not another deck.

method_note: Scoring blends creative repetition, format diversity, copy variance, and angle saturation against luxury womenswear benchmarks; no impression or spend data was available for this forecast.
